title: One-shot MDA-gated FE candidate proposer (scoped variant of B0024)
source: DA verdict on B0024 ([review](backlog_reviews/20260526-two-level-fe-generator-proposal_v1.json)) §reviewer_notes — "viable scoped form: one-shot exploratory script proposing top-K candidates by MDA on a single fold, gated through human review". Originally inspired by 2026-05-26 review of Optiver Kaggle two-level FE.
why: Hand-curated tier2 features almost certainly miss productive interactions, but the open-ended generator form (B0024) overfits on 5,500 bars / ~400 events/fold. The bounded form is a one-shot exploratory script proposing a handful of causally-audited candidates ranked by MDA, surfaced to a human reviewer who decides whether each enters [pipeline/features.py](pipeline/features.py). Discipline is the value, not the raw alpha.
value_rationale: L-M (small additive; primary value is the discipline of MDA-gated + causality-tested FE expansion — not raw alpha)
scope: reads `mda_per_fold.json` for the chosen primary, aggregates across folds, identifies top-5 features by median MDA

generates O(top-5 × top-5) cross-products (≤25 candidates) — ratios, spreads, products — restricted to **macro × technical pairs only** (DA must_have_mod #2: not technical × technical, which are near-collinear on daily OHLC)
applies `tests/test_feature_causality.py` (precondition, see below) to filter any candidate with leaky propagation
ranks survivors by single-fold inner-CV MDA (NEVER test-fold), emits top-K (K≤10) markdown report for human review

new `tests/test_feature_causality.py` (DA must_have_mod #3, **mandatory precondition** — no candidate enters `features.py` without passing): asserts each candidate at bar `t` depends only on inputs ≤ `t-1` for macro and ≤ `t` for technical.

**pre-registered acceptance criterion** (DA must_have_mod #1): a candidate is merged into `build_tier2_features` ONLY if the human reviewer pre-registers an improvement threshold (e.g. median OOS Sharpe across folds for the chosen primary improves by ≥0.3 with `min_trades` held) AND a held-out tail-window confirms it. Hard ceiling: ≤10 candidates surfaced per run.
